title: calais glauca
intro: Calais glauca, also known as Marsh Marigold, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow or orange flowers and shiny green leaves. The medicinal properties of Calais glauca include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and skin irritations. Calais glauca can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
